stimulus: After the city of Reykjavík began charging a small fee on disposable coffee cups in 2017, single-use cup consumption fell by nearly seventy ______blank fees are subject to what economists call the "habituation effect": once shoppers adjusted, cup use slowly started to rise again.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. It appropriately uses a period to mark the end of one independent clause ("After...nearly seventy percent") and the start of another ("Such fees...to rise again").

Choice B is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Choice C is incorrect because a comma is needed in addition to the conjunction "and" when joining two independent clauses. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a run-on sentence.
